mnemonics: 10 SAMPLES = 10 DIFFERENT FRIENDS. Each friend gives you a different opinion (estimate) on the same topic (population), and you need to consider them all to get the full picture.
keyPoints: Taking multiple samples helps understand the sampling distribution of an estimator.
Different samples will yield different estimates, illustrating the concept of estimator variance.
The variability between samples is crucial for constructing confidence intervals and understanding the stability of your model's predictions.
commonMistakes: Believing a single sample's estimate is the definitive true population parameter.
Ignoring the variability between samples, leading to overconfident conclusions.
Confusing the variation within a single sample with the variation between different samples.
